title: Boat Rental in Lake Maggiore: A 2026 Guide to Exploring by Water
html:
Lake Maggiore offers some of Italy's most scenic boating, with departures from Stresa letting you explore the Borromean Islands and beyond. A half-day small motorboat charter typically costs from 250 EUR, and BoatHire24 makes it easy to book a charter online for your trip.

Where to set off in Lake Maggiore

Stresa is the most popular starting point, with Marina di Stresa offering berths and easy access to the lake. Arona, at the southern end, has Porto di Arona with good facilities. At the northern end, Cannobio's port is ideal for exploring the Swiss border. The Borromean Islands, Isola Bella, Isola Madre and Isola dei Pescatori, are all within short cruises. Baveno and Verbania also have reputable marinas.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (up to 6 guests) Best for island hopping. From 250 EUR for a half day.
  2. Rib speedboat (up to 8 guests) Ideal for watersports and speed. From 350 EUR half day.
  3. Deck boat (up to 10 guests) Good for family picnics and swimming stops. From 450 EUR half day.
  4. Sailing yacht (up to 12 guests) Perfect for a relaxing full-day cruise. From 600 EUR full day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (up to 12 guests) Includes crew and premium amenities. From 1,200 EUR full day.

For a sporty option, consider the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire, which combines agility with comfort for small groups.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Maggiore?

Prices range from 40 EUR per person for a shared tour to 2000 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht full-day charter.

  • Shared group tour: 40, 80 EUR per person (2, 3 hours)
  • Small motorboat half-day: 250, 400 EUR (up to 6 guests)
  • Catamaran full-day: 700, 1000 EUR (up to 12 guests)
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day: 1200, 2000 EUR (up to 12 guests)

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Maggiore?

In Italian waters, a licence is required for boats over 30 kW (40 hp), but many rental operators offer licence-free boats. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator before booking. For larger vessels, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is typically accepted. The warmest months for boating are June to September, when the prevailing wind is the 'Inverna' from the north. The lake is navigable year-round, but services reduce outside this window.

A local tip: head to Isola dei Pescatori in the late afternoon for a quiet anchorage and a glass of wine at a waterfront restaurant.

How to book your boat rental

Using BoatHire24, you can compare verified listings, read reviews, and book securely. Simply enter your dates and group size, then filter by boat type and price. Use the boat search to see all available vessels for Lake Maggiore, with instant booking on many options.

At a glance

  • Price range per half day: 250, 2000 EUR
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 1 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Popular departure: Stresa, distance to Isola Bella: 1.5 nautical miles

Questions about boat rental in Lake Maggiore

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Lake Maggiore?

Yes, many operators offer smaller motorboats under 40 hp that do not require a licence.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Lake Maggiore?

The best period is June to September, when the weather is warm and all marinas are fully operational.

Are pets allowed on boat rentals in Lake Maggiore?

Some operators allow well-behaved pets, but you must check the listing's pet policy before booking.

Can I rent a boat for a half day only?

Yes, many boats are available for half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours) charters.

Whether you are exploring the Borromean Islands or the western shore with its grand villas, renting a boat gives you freedom on the water. BoatHire24 connects you with owners who list your boat for thousands of travellers each season. For owners, it is a simple way to earn income from your vessel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake Maggiore and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
